title: Apodokimos Weight Function Specification
description: Machine-readable specification for W(c,t) = R(t) × D × S × O (P-03 to P-07)

weight_function.formula: W(c, t) = R(c, t) × D(c) × S(c) × O(c)

domain.W: [0, ∞) — epistemic weight of claim c at time t

normalization.formula: W_norm(c, t, field) = W(c, t) / μ_field(t)
normalization.mu_field: Running mean W across all claims in same field at time t

R_factor.description: P-04: Replication score with field-calibrated time decay
R_factor.formula: R(c, t) = Σ w_decay(t - a.block, λ_field) / (|Replicates| + |Refutes| + ε)

epsilon.description: Small positive constant preventing division by zero

D_factor.description: P-05: Dependency depth — DAG traversal specification

algorithm.name: Transitive closure of dependency relation
steps: For each claim c, perform DFS/BFS to find all transitive dependents
D(c) = count of claims that depend on c directly or transitively
algorithm.complexity: O(V + E) per query with memoization
algorithm.cycle_check: Performed at registration: reject if c ∈ ancestors(c) for any dependency

D_factor.rationale: Captures structural importance independent of citation velocity

S_factor.description: P-06: Survival rate — ratio of supporting to total non-Mentions attestations

interpretation.S_near_1: Claim replicated 10+ times with no contradictions

oracle_sources.aggregation: O = max(source_scores) — multiple sources, take highest

O_factor.rationale: O = 0 does not penalize basic science; O > 0 signals real-world consequence
